{"step_by_step_thinking": "Resistance training is well-documented in the provided documents as a key factor in increasing muscle hypertrophy. Multiple sources confirm that resistance training promotes muscle growth by increasing protein synthesis and cross-sectional area of muscle fibers. For example, Document [0] states that resistance training increases protein synthesis and that myonuclei increase in size to support greater protein synthesis and muscle fiber growth. Document [5] highlights that resistance training effectively attenuates muscle loss and improves strength in sarcopenia, which is directly linked to muscle hypertrophy. Document [9] notes that resistance training increases resting metabolic rate and improves insulin sensitivity, which are outcomes of increased muscle mass. Document [7] and [19] emphasize the use of resistance training to improve muscle strength and mass. Additionally, Document [20] and [31] state that resistance exercise increases protein synthesis and reduces muscle degradation, further supporting muscle hypertrophy.
